page.title: Astondoa 40 vs Pershing 46: Which Yacht Suits Your Marbella Charter?

data.meta_description: Compare Astondoa 40 vs Pershing 46 for your Marbella charter. See specs, capacity, pricing, and which suits your group. Book now.
data.summary: A detailed comparison of the Astondoa 40 and Pershing 46 for Marbella charters, covering size, capacity, pricing, and ideal use cases to help you choose.
key_facts: Astondoa 40 starts at €749 for 2 hours (skipper, drinks, VAT incl.).
Pershing 46 pricing on request via WhatsApp.
Both include licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and IVA.
Astondoa 40 best for budget-friendly, shorter trips.
Pershing 46 better for larger groups and longer cruises.
Both operate from Puerto Banús, Marbella's prime marina.

faq.q: Which is bigger, Astondoa 40 or Pershing 46?
faq.a: The Pershing 46 is larger at 14 metres (46 ft) compared to the Astondoa 40 at 12.5 metres (40 ft). The Pershing offers more deck space and a higher passenger capacity (10 vs 9).

faq.q: Can I book the Pershing 46 for a 2-hour trip?
faq.a: Pricing for the Pershing 46 is on request, so you can ask for shorter durations. However, the Astondoa 40 has a fixed rate of €749 for 2 hours, making it a more straightforward option for quick trips.

faq.q: What's included in the Astondoa 40 charter price?
faq.a: The Astondoa 40 rate includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden costs.

faq.q: Which yacht is better for a group of 10?
faq.a: The Pershing 46 can take up to 10 passengers, while the Astondoa 40 is capped at 9. For a full group of 10, choose the Pershing 46. For 9 or fewer, the Astondoa 40 works well.

faq.q: Do both yachts depart from the same marina?
faq.a: Yes, both the Astondoa 40 and Pershing 46 depart from Puerto Banús in Marbella. This makes them equally convenient for exploring the Costa del Sol coastline.

faq.q: Is the Pershing 46 faster than the Astondoa 40?
faq.a: Both are motor yachts, but exact speeds are not specified. The Pershing 46 is a performance-oriented model known for speed, while the Astondoa 40 offers a comfortable cruise. Contact us for specific performance details.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food on either yacht?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food. Each charter includes complimentary drinks and light snacks. Catering can also be arranged on request for both yachts.

faq.q: How do I book the Pershing 46?
faq.a: To book the Pershing 46, contact us via WhatsApp for pricing and availability. The Astondoa 40 can be booked directly online with fixed rates starting at €749 for 2 hours.

data.body_html:
Choosing between the Astondoa 40 and Pershing 46 for your Marbella charter? Both depart from Puerto Banús and offer premium experiences, but they suit different budgets and group sizes. This comparison breaks down the key differences in size, capacity, pricing, and inclusions to help you decide which yacht fits your day on the Costa del Sol.

Size and Capacity

The Astondoa 40 measures 12.5 metres and accommodates up to 9 passengers. The Pershing 46 is larger at 14 metres, with a maximum capacity of 10 guests. For groups of 10, the Pershing is the only option; for 9 or fewer, the Astondoa offers ample space. Both yachts provide sunbathing areas, a saloon, and a small galley, but the Pershing's extra length gives slightly more deck room.

Pricing and Value

The Astondoa 40 has transparent pricing: €749 for 2 hours, scaling up to €2,299 for 8 hours. This includes a skipper, fuel, drinks (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava), light snacks, insurance, and Spanish IVA. The Pershing 46 is priced on request via WhatsApp, making it less straightforward for budget planning but potentially negotiable for longer charters. For cost certainty, the Astondoa 40 wins.

Inclusions and Amenities

Both charters include the same standard package: licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and 21% IVA. No hidden extras. The Pershing 46 may offer more premium finishes, but the core inclusions are identical. You can bring your own food on either yacht, and catering is available on request.

Performance and Style

The Pershing 46 is known for sporty lines and higher performance, appealing to those who want a faster, more dynamic ride. The Astondoa 40 is a classic flybridge cruiser, prioritising comfort and stability at cruising speeds. Both are well-maintained and depart from Puerto Banús, giving you direct access to Marbella Bay, Cabopino, and Estepona.

Ideal Use Cases

Choose the Astondoa 40 if: you have 9 or fewer guests, want fixed pricing from €749, and prefer a simple, all-inclusive booking. Perfect for a half-day trip to Cala del Faro or a sunset cruise along the Golden Mile.
Choose the Pershing 46 if: your group is 10 people, you value a sportier design, and you're flexible on budget. Ideal for full-day excursions to Sotogrande or longer coastal tours.

Which Departs from Puerto Banús?

Both yachts operate from Puerto Banús, the iconic marina in Marbella. This means easy access to restaurants, shops, and parking. Whether you choose the Astondoa 40 or Pershing 46, you'll start your charter from the heart of the Costa del Sol's luxury scene. Explore our full [fleet](/boats/) for more options.

Frequently asked questions

Which is bigger, Astondoa 40 or Pershing 46?

The Pershing 46 is larger at 14 metres (46 ft) compared to the Astondoa 40 at 12.5 metres (40 ft). The Pershing offers more deck space and a higher passenger capacity (10 vs 9).

Can I book the Pershing 46 for a 2-hour trip?

Pricing for the Pershing 46 is on request, so you can ask for shorter durations. However, the Astondoa 40 has a fixed rate of €749 for 2 hours, making it a more straightforward option for quick trips.

What's included in the Astondoa 40 charter price?

The Astondoa 40 rate includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden costs.

Which yacht is better for a group of 10?

The Pershing 46 can take up to 10 passengers, while the Astondoa 40 is capped at 9. For a full group of 10, choose the Pershing 46. For 9 or fewer, the Astondoa 40 works well.

Do both yachts depart from the same marina?

Yes, both the Astondoa 40 and Pershing 46 depart from Puerto Banús in Marbella. This makes them equally convenient for exploring the Costa del Sol coastline.

Is the Pershing 46 faster than the Astondoa 40?

Both are motor yachts, but exact speeds are not specified. The Pershing 46 is a performance-oriented model known for speed, while the Astondoa 40 offers a comfortable cruise. Contact us for specific performance details.

Can I bring my own food on either yacht?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food. Each charter includes complimentary drinks and light snacks. Catering can also be arranged on request for both yachts.

How do I book the Pershing 46?

To book the Pershing 46, contact us via WhatsApp for pricing and availability. The Astondoa 40 can be booked directly online with fixed rates starting at €749 for 2 hours.
